methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $178M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Heron Power is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Drew Baglino that has raised $178M+ to date. The valuation analysis relies on a peer set of five same-stage, same-sector private companies—Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita—supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA), reflecting the early-stage nature of the frontier-tech climate sector.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses total capital raised ($178M) as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $338M. The peer-median method examines five climate and energy companies at Series B, which span $120M to $500M in valuation with a median of $310M. This range reflects material dispersion within the cohort; Pano AI and Sage Geosystems command $500M and $400M respectively, while Cemvita trades at $120M.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $264M to $389M, with a median of $324M. The relatively tight alignment between stage-progression ($338M) and peer-median ($310M) lends credibility to the central estimate. Key uncertainties include Heron's relative technology maturity versus peers, commercialization timeline, and capital efficiency relative to the $120M–$500M dispersion observed in the peer set.
